content.description:
The REPL (Read-Eval-Print Loop) is Hobson's interactive JavaScript console for querying, scripting, and extending the system at runtime.

## Overview

The REPL provides direct access to Hobson's API, letting you:

- Query and inspect items in storage
- Create, modify, and delete items
- Test code before packaging it as libraries or views
- Debug renderers and system behavior
- Import and export data

## Accessing the REPL

| Action | Keyboard |
|--------|----------|
| Toggle REPL | `Ctrl+\` |

The REPL appears as a collapsible panel at the bottom of the viewport. It persists across page reloads.

## The `api` Object

The REPL provides an `api` object with full access to the system:

![](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000?symbol=Kernel.createAPI)

```query
const core = await api.get('33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000');
const symbols = core.content._symbols || {};

// Filter to REPL API methods (direct children only)
const apiMethods = Object.entries(symbols)
  .filter(([key, info]) => {
    return key.startsWith('Kernel.createAPI.') &&
           key.split('.').length === 3 &&
           info.kind.includes('function');
  })
  .sort((a, b) => a[1].line - b[1].line);

// Generate markdown table with linked method names
let md = '| Method | Signature |\n|--------|----------|\n';
for (const [key, info] of apiMethods) {
  // Put full method name inside the link text to avoid markdown parsing issues
  const link = `[\`api.${info.name}\`](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000?symbol=${key})`;
  md += `| ${link} | \`${info.signature || '()'}\` |\n`;
}
return md;
```


```javascript
// Storage operations                  
await api.get(id)                // Get item by ID
await api.set(item)              // Save item (no re-render)
await api.update(item)           // Save item and re-render
await api.delete(id)             // Delete item
await api.query({ name: 'foo' }) // Find items by properties
await api.getAll()               // Get all items

// Code loading
await api.require('library-name') // Load a library by name

// Navigation
await api.navigate(itemId)        // Navigate to item
await api.navigate(id, { field: 'code', line: 42 }) // With scroll target

// Rendering
await api.renderItem(itemId)      // Render an item to DOM
await api.rerenderItem(itemId)    // Re-render visible instances
await api.getViews(typeId)        // Get available views for type

// Events
api.events.on(eventId, handler)   // Subscribe to events
api.events.emit({ type, content }) // Emit event

// Import/Export
await api.import()                // Import items from JSON file
await api.export()                // Export all items to JSON
```

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Ctrl+Enter` | Run code |
| `Escape` | Dismiss REPL |
| `Up/Down` | Navigate history |
| `Tab` | Insert indentation |

## Examples

### Find an item by name
```javascript
const item = await api.helpers.findByName('my-library');
return item;
```

### Create a new note
```javascript
await api.set({
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: 'My Note',
  type: '871ae771-b9b1-4f40-8c7f-d9038bfb69c3', // note type
  attachments: [],
  content: { description: 'Hello world' }
});
```

### List all views
```javascript
const views = await api.query({ type: api.IDS.VIEW });
return views.map(v => v.name);
```

### Navigate to the viewport
```javascript
await api.navigate(api.IDS.VIEWPORT);
```

### Check render instances
```javascript
return api.instances.getSummary();
```

## Architecture

The REPL has two parts:

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| REPL API | [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) | The `api` object and `createAPI()` function |
| REPL UI | [repl-ui](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) | Collapsible panel, transcript, history |

The API is defined in the kernel because it's used everywhere—not just in the REPL console, but also in renderers, views, and watch handlers.

## REPL API vs Renderer API

Renderers receive a subset of the REPL API tailored for rendering:

| Feature | REPL API | Renderer API |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| `api.get/set/update` | Yes | Yes |
| `api.require` | Yes | Yes |
| `api.navigate` | Yes | Yes |
| `api.import/export` | Yes | No |
| `api.delete` | Yes | No |
| `siblingContainer` | No | Yes (if in container) |

## Tips

- **Multiline code**: The input is a textarea—write functions, loops, anything
- **Async/await**: Top-level `await` works automatically
- **Auto-return**: Single expressions auto-return their result (e.g. `await api.get(id)` just works)
- **Explicit return**: For multi-statement code, use `return` on the last expression
- **No return**: Multi-statement code without `return` executes silently (useful for side effects)
- **String escaping**: When creating code items, be careful with nested quotes

## Common Gotchas

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| "Module not found" | Check the library name matches exactly |
| Changes not visible | Call `api.renderViewport()` or use `api.update()` |
| Code item not updating | Call `api.require()` again—modules are cached |

## See Also

- [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000) — Where the REPL API is defined
- [repl-ui](item://f1111111-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — The REPL panel implementation
- [Import/Export System](item://c0c0c0c0-0027-0000-0000-000000000000) — `api.import()` and `api.export()` details
- [concept:safe-mode](item://33333333-7777-0000-0000-000000000000) — Recovery when code breaks (REPL disabled in safe mode)
